role_title: Senior Product Manager, Pet Claims Experience

level_signals: 5+ years of product management experience required

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: At least 5 years of product management experience at a startup or fast-growing company - a must
must_haves.qualifier: at a startup or fast-growing company

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: A track record of untangling complex processes and finding the clearest path through them
Strong UX instincts with an eye for detail and an ability to hold the big picture at the same time
A data-driven, analytical approach to decisions and prioritization
Top-notch written and verbal communication skills in English
A genuine drive to keep learning - you read constantly and love sharing what you find
Enthusiasm for AI and a real commitment to growing alongside these tools as they evolve
Ready to work in an office environment most days of the week

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience automating manual processes - a big plus

scope.surface: End-to-end pet insurance claims journey, from first notice of loss to resolution
primary_metrics_inferred: Customer Ops efficiency / manual work reduction
core_responsibilities: Own the full pet claims journey, from first notice of loss to resolution, and raise the bar on what that experience can be
Design and ship automation solutions that reduce manual work, scale Customer Ops, and move the efficiency needle in a meaningful way
Put GenAI and LLMs to work using Lemonade's internal AI tooling to rethink what's possible in claims handling
Translate product strategy into sharp requirements and prototypes that reflect Lemonade's brand, mission, and customer needs
Partner with designers, engineers, data analysts, and Customer Ops stakeholders to ship end-to-end product improvements
Identify gaps, prioritize ruthlessly, and bring creative solutions that expand what our product can do

green_flags: Clear product ownership scope (end-to-end pet claims journey)
AI-first culture with concrete tooling (internal AI tooling, GenAI, LLMs) — not just aspirational
Explicitly names cross-functional partners, giving clarity on collaboration model
Company is a known insurtech brand with established product culture
red_flags: No city specified for the onsite role — candidates cannot determine which office location applies
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not mentioned
No mention of equity or benefits
'Experience leading small, agile teams' is in the required section but reads more like a nice-to-have — severity classification is ambiguous

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Senior Product Manager' and 5+ years requirement)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false; 'leading teams' is cross-functional, not people management)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.type (inferred as onsite from 'work in an office environment most days of the week')
ambiguities: 'Experience leading small, agile teams' appears in the 'What you'll need' section alongside hard requirements, but does not carry a 'must' qualifier. Classified as hard given its placement, but could be a soft requirement.
No specific city or country is mentioned for the office location, making it unclear where this role is based.

'Experience automating manual processes - a big plus' uses 'big plus' language suggesting it is a nice-to-have, but also could be interpreted as a strong preference given automation is central to the role's responsibilities.
